title: The Homogenization of Game Design: How AI-Driven Development Is Killing Creativity
summary: This paper critically examines how the widespread adoption of AI-driven development tools is contributing to a growing homogenisation of game design, arguing that while AI technologies such as procedural generation and automated asset creation have improved production efficiency, they have simultaneously reduced the diversity of artistic expression and stifled creative innovation across the industry. The paper identifies three core concerns: AI-generated content is causing visual aesthetics and mechanical structures to converge into predictable, formulaic patterns; delegating design decisions to AI undermines human creative agency and produces narratives that are structurally coherent but emotionally shallow; and approximately 78% of triple-A studios now rely on AI-based generation techniques, displacing skilled artists, writers, and designers while raising unresolved questions around authorship and intellectual property. The paper concludes that AI, if left unchecked, threatens both the creative integrity and economic stability of the games industry, and calls for future research into frameworks that position AI as an assistive tool rather than a replacement for human creativity.
